# Mars High Astrocartography

First mentioned: **3302-04-08**  
Last mentioned: **3302-12-23**

## Biography

Mars High Astrocartography operated as one of the Federation's premier deep-space mapping institutions during 3302, producing cartographic data that supported expeditions to the galaxy's most remote regions. The organisation's work encompassed the Formidine Rift, the Norma Expanse and Beagle Point — destinations that lay beyond the range of conventional survey missions and required specialised long-range cartographic techniques.

Though connected to Sirius Corporation and the Sol-based scientific establishment, Mars High Astrocartography maintained operational independence that allowed it to collaborate with civilian expedition organisers. Its eight-month period of documented activity corresponded with the great exploration boom of 3302, when independent pilots pushed further into uncharted space than ever before. The organisation's contributions to galactic cartography remained foundational, providing the baseline navigational data that subsequent generations of explorers would build upon.
